{"content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Build the Gap Analysis Memorandum (.docx) for the Vantage Logistics Group
multi-state paid leave compliance review (Hartwell & Cabot LLP engagement).
"""
from docx import Document
from docx.shared import Pt, Inches, RGBColor, Cm
from docx.enum.text import WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH, WD_LINE_SPACING
from docx.enum.table import WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT, WD_ALIGN_VERTICAL
from docx.enum.section import WD_SECTION
from docx.oxml.ns import qn
from docx.oxml import OxmlElement

NAVY = RGBColor(0x1F, 0x38, 0x64)
STEEL = RGBColor(0x2E, 0x53, 0x95)
GREY = RGBColor(0x59, 0x59, 0x59)
RED = RGBColor(0x9C, 0x00, 0x06)
DKYEL = RGBColor(0x9C, 0x65, 0x00)
DKGRN = RGBColor(0x00, 0x61, 0x00)
WHITE = RGBColor(0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF)
BLACK = RGBColor(0x00, 0x00, 0x00)

RED_HEX = "FFC7CE"; YEL_HEX = "FFEB9C"; GRN_HEX = "C6EFCE"
NAVY_HEX = "1F3864"; STEEL_HEX = "2E5395"; LBLUE_HEX = "D9E1F2"; LGREY_HEX = "F2F2F2"


def shade(cell, hex_color):
    tcPr = cell._tc.get_or_add_tcPr()
    shd = OxmlElement("w:shd")
    shd.set(qn("w:val"), "clear")
    shd.set(qn("w:color"), "auto")
    shd.set(qn("w:fill"), hex_color)
    tcPr.append(shd)


def set_cell_margins(cell, top=40, bottom=40, left=80, right=80):
    tcPr = cell._tc.get_or_add_tcPr()
    m = OxmlElement("w:tcMar")
    for tag, val in (("top", top), ("bottom", bottom), ("start", left), ("end", right)):
        e = OxmlElement(f"w:{tag}")
        e.set(qn("w:w"), str(val))
        e.set(qn("w:type"), "dxa")
        m.append(e)
    tcPr.append(m)


def set_repeat_header(row):
    trPr = row._tr.get_or_add_trPr()
    th = OxmlElement("w:tblHeader")
    th.set(qn("w:val"), "true")
    trPr.append(th)


def no_space(p):
    p.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(0)
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(0)
    p.paragraph_format.line_spacing = 1.0


def add_run(p, text, size=10.5, bold=False, italic=False, color=BLACK, font="Calibri"):
    r = p.add_run(text)
    r.font.name = font
    r.font.size = Pt(size)
    r.bold = bold
    r.italic = italic
    r.font.color.rgb = color
    return r


def heading(doc, text, level=1):
    p = doc.add_paragraph()
    p.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(14 if level == 1 else 10)
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(4)
    p.paragraph_format.keep_with_next = True
    if level == 1:
        add_run(p, text, size=12.5, bold=True, color=NAVY)
        # bottom border
        pPr = p._p.get_or_add_pPr()
        pbdr = OxmlElement("w:pBdr")
        bottom = OxmlElement("w:bottom")
        bottom.set(qn("w:val"), "single"); bottom.set(qn("w:sz"), "6")
        bottom.set(qn("w:space"), "2"); bottom.set(qn("w:color"), "1F3864")
        pbdr.append(bottom); pPr.append(pbdr)
    else:
        add_run(p, text, size=11, bold=True, color=STEEL)
    return p


def body(doc, text, size=10.5, space_after=6, justify=True):
    p = doc.add_paragraph()
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(space_after)
    p.paragraph_format.line_spacing = 1.08
    if justify:
        p.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.JUSTIFY
    add_run(p, text, size=size)
    return p


def bullet(doc, text, level=0, size=10.5):
    p = doc.add_paragraph(style="List Bullet")
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(3)
    p.paragraph_format.line_spacing = 1.05
    p.paragraph_format.left_indent = Inches(0.3 + 0.25 * level)
    add_run(p, text, size=size)
    return p


def numbered(doc, text, size=10.5):
    p = doc.add_paragraph(style="List Number")
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(3)
    p.paragraph_format.line_spacing = 1.05
    add_run(p, text, size=size)
    return p


def labeled_bullet(doc, label, text, size=10.5):
    p = doc.add_paragraph(style="List Bullet")
    p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(3)
    p.paragraph_format.line_spacing = 1.05
    add_run(p, label, size=size, bold=True)
    add_run(p, text, size=size)
    return p


# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
def build():
    doc = Document()

    # Base style
    normal = doc.styles["Normal"]
    normal.font.name = "Calibri"
    normal.font.size = Pt(10.5)

    # Margins
    for s in doc.sections:
        s.top_margin = Inches(0.8)
        s.bottom_margin = Inches(0.8)
        s.left_margin = Inches(0.9)
        s.right_margin = Inches(0.9)

    # ---- Letterhead ----
    p = doc.add_paragraph()
    p.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
    no_space(p)
    add_run(p, "HARTWELL & CABOT LLP", size=18, bold=True, color=NAVY)
    p2 = doc.add_paragraph()
    p2.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
    no_space(p2)
    add_run(p2, "Attorneys at Law", size=10, italic=True, color=GREY)
    p3 = doc.add_paragraph()
    p3.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
    p3.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(2)
    add_run(p3, "One Gateway Center, Suite 2400, Newark, New Jersey 07102  |  225 Franklin Street, 18th Floor, Boston, Massachusetts 02110", size=8, color=GREY)

    # Privilege bar
    pbar = doc.add_paragraph()
    pbar.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
    pbar.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(4)
    pbar.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(8)
    add_run(pbar, "PRIVILEGED & CONFIDENTIAL  |  ATTORNEY-CLIENT COMMUNICATION  |  ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT", size=8.5, bold=True, color=RED)

    # Horizontal rule
    hr = doc.add_paragraph()
    hr.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(6)
    pPr = hr._p.get_or_add_pPr()
    pbdr = OxmlElement("w:pBdr")
    bottom = OxmlElement("w:bottom")
    bottom.set(qn("w:val"), "single"); bottom.set(qn("w:sz"), "12")
    bottom.set(qn("w:space"), "1"); bottom.set(qn("w:color"), "1F3864")
    pbdr.append(bottom); pPr.append(pbdr)

    # ---- Memo header block ----
    title = doc.add_paragraph()
    title.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
    title.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(8)
    add_run(title, "GAP ANALYSIS AND REMEDIATION MEMORANDUM", size=14, bold=True, color=NAVY)

    hdr = [
        ("TO:", "Rebecca Thorsen, Esq., Senior Vice President & General Counsel, Vantage Logistics Group, Inc."),
        ("FROM:", "Daniel Ikeda, Senior Associate  |  Catherine Ng, Partner (supervising)"),
        ("DATE:", "May 19, 2025"),
        ("RE:", "Multi-State Paid Leave Compliance Review — Gap Analysis, Risk Assessment, and Prioritized Remediation Plan (NJ, NY, CT, MA, CO, OR, WA)"),
    ]
    t = doc.add_table(rows=len(hdr), cols=2)
    t.allow_autofit = False
    t.columns[0].width = Inches(0.8)
    t.columns[1].width = Inches(5.9)
    for i, (lab, val) in enumerate(hdr):
        c0 = t.cell(i, 0); c1 = t.cell(i, 1)
        c0.width = Inches(0.8); c1.width = Inches(5.9)
        pp = c0.paragraphs[0]; no_space(pp); add_run(pp, lab, size=10, bold=True, color=NAVY)
        pp2 = c1.paragraphs[0]; no_space(pp2); add_run(pp2, val, size=10)
        set_cell_margins(c0, top=20, bottom=20); set_cell_margins(c1, top=20, bottom=20)

    hr2 = doc.add_paragraph()
    hr2.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(4)
    hr2.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(8)
    pPr = hr2._p.get_or_add_pPr()
    pbdr = OxmlElement("w:pBdr")
    bottom = OxmlElement("w:bottom")
    bottom.set(qn("w:val"), "single"); bottom.set(qn("w:sz"), "6")
    bottom.set(qn("w:space"), "1"); bottom.set(qn("w:color"), "BFBFBF")
    pbdr.append(bottom); pPr.append(pbdr)

    # ===================== I. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY =====================
    heading(doc, "I.  Executive Summary", 1)
    body(doc,
         "Hartwell & Cabot LLP (\"H&C\" or the \"Firm\") has completed the multi-state paid leave compliance review "
         "of Vantage Logistics Group, Inc. (\"Vantage\" or the \"Company\") commissioned under the engagement letter "
         "dated April 14, 2025. This memorandum, together with the accompanying Multi-State Compliance Matrix "
         "(\"the Matrix\"), identifies all material compliance deficiencies between the Company's current paid leave "
         "policies and practices—as set forth in the Employee Handbook (Rev. January 15, 2022) and as implemented in "
         "practice—and the statutory and regulatory requirements of the seven jurisdictions in which the Company "
         "operates or plans to operate: New Jersey, New York, and Connecticut (current operations), and Massachusetts, "
         "Colorado, Oregon, and Washington (planned expansion). Each deficiency is classified by severity—Critical, "
         "High, or Moderate—and is accompanied by a prioritized remediation plan tied to the Company's facility opening "
         "schedule and the July 1, 2025 target date for a revised Employee Handbook.")
    body(doc,
         "The review identifies systemic, cross-jurisdictional non-compliance. Of the 210 dimension-level comparisons "
         "captured in the Matrix, 140 are non-compliant (red), 31 are at risk (yellow), and 39 are compliant (green). "
         "The Company's paid sick leave policy is non-compliant in all seven states; its self-funded paid family leave "
         "plan falls below the statutory minimum in all seven states and has never been approved as a private-plan "
         "substitute in any jurisdiction; and the Company provides no employer-funded paid medical leave for an "
         "employee's own serious health condition in any state, even though employees in New Jersey, New York, and "
         "Connecticut are already funding state programs through payroll withholdings. The Company has not registered "
         "with the paid leave insurance program in any of the four expansion states, and its PTO payout policy is "
         "unlawful in four of the seven jurisdictions. The Company also faces an active enforcement matter before the "
         "New Jersey Department of Labor (Case No. NJ-ESL-2025-04117) that has already exposed the core deficiencies "
         "in its sick leave policy.")
    body(doc,
         "Four items warrant immediate attention as Critical: (1) Massachusetts PFML registration, which must be "
         "completed by approximately June 1, 2025 to allow PinnacleHR Solutions, Inc. (\"PinnacleHR\") its six-to-eight-"
         "week configuration lead time before the August 1, 2025 facility opening; (2) Colorado FAMLI registration, "
         "which may already be triggered by the three remote Colorado employees hired in April 2025 and for which no "
         "private-employer opt-out exists; (3) the New Jersey Earned Sick Leave Act violations underlying the Muñoz "
         "complaint; and (4) the structural inadequacy of the self-funded paid family leave plan across all seven "
         "jurisdictions. H&C has flagged the Massachusetts and Colorado registration urgency separately and in advance "
         "of this deliverable, consistent with the engagement letter.")

    # ===================== II. SCOPE & METHODOLOGY =====================
    heading(doc, "II.  Scope, Methodology, and Limitations", 1)
    body(doc,
         "Scope. The review covers five dimensions for each jurisdiction: (1) paid sick leave; (2) paid family leave; "
         "(3) paid medical leave (an employee's own serious health condition); (4) employer obligations (state program "
         "registration, contribution rates and wage bases, payroll withholding, recordkeeping, and workplace posting "
         "and individual notice); and (5) PTO/vacation payout on separation. The Matrix presents the governing state "
         "requirement for each dimension alongside the Company's current policy or practice, with color-coded gap "
         "indicators (red / yellow / green).")
    body(doc,
         "Methodology. H&C analyzed the Employee Handbook (Rev. January 15, 2022), the PinnacleHR payroll withholding "
         "summary dated April 10, 2025, the HR leave-tracking sample, the NJ DOL complaint correspondence (Case No. "
         "NJ-ESL-2025-04117), and internal HR correspondence between Marcus Delgado and Rebecca Thorsen. H&C applied "
         "the statutes, regulations, and agency guidance in effect as of May 19, 2025 for each jurisdiction. Where the "
         "Company's written policy is silent or ambiguous, H&C assessed the policy against the information provided "
         "regarding actual practice.")
    body(doc,
         "Limitations. This memorandum is based on the accuracy and completeness of the information and representations "
         "provided by the Company. State paid leave law is a rapidly evolving area; legislative or regulatory changes "
         "after the deliverable date may affect the analysis. The review does not address federal FMLA compliance, "
         "local or municipal paid leave ordinances, workers' compensation, or unemployment insurance, each of which is "
         "outside the engagement scope. This memorandum does not constitute a guarantee of any particular compliance "
         "outcome and does not constitute representation of the Company in the pending NJ DOL matter, which continues "
         "under the separate engagement dated February 15, 2025.")

    # ===================== III. SUMMARY OF COMPLIANCE POSTURE =====================
    heading(doc, "III.  Summary of Compliance Posture", 1)
    body(doc,
         "The table below summarizes the overall compliance posture by category and jurisdiction. The figures count "
         "dimension-level comparisons within each category. Detailed findings appear in Sections IV through VI and in "
         "the Gap Register tab of the Matrix.")

    # Summary table
    cats = [
        ("Paid Sick Leave", ["R", "R", "R", "R", "R", "R", "R"]),
        ("Paid Family Leave", ["R", "R", "R", "R", "R", "R", "R"]),
        ("Paid Medical Leave", ["R", "R", "R", "R", "R", "R", "R"]),
        ("Employer Obligations", ["Y", "Y", "Y", "R", "R", "R", "R"]),
        ("PTO Payout on Separation", ["G", "G", "G", "R", "R", "R", "R"]),
    ]
    states = ["NJ", "NY", "CT", "MA", "CO", "OR", "WA"]
    word = {"R": "Non-compliant", "Y": "At risk", "G": "Compliant"}
    fill = {"R": RED_HEX, "Y": YEL_HEX, "G": GRN_HEX}
    fcolor = {"R": RED, "Y": DKYEL, "G": DKGRN}

    tbl = doc.add_table(rows=1 + len(cats) + 1, cols=9)
    tbl.alignment = WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT.CENTER
    tbl.style = "Table Grid"
    widths = [Inches(1.7)] + [Inches(0.62)] * 7 + [Inches(0.9)]
    # header
    hcells = ["Category"] + states + ["R/Y/G"]
    for j, h in enumerate(hcells):
        c = tbl.cell(0, j); c.width = widths[j]
        shade(c, NAVY_HEX); set_cell_margins(c)
        pp = c.paragraphs[0]; pp.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER; no_space(pp)
        add_run(pp, h, size=9, bold=True, color=WHITE)
    set_repeat_header(tbl.rows[0])
    # rows
    for i, (cat, stvals) in enumerate(cats):
        r = i + 1
        c = tbl.cell(r, 0); c.width = widths[0]; shade(c, LBLUE_HEX); set_cell_margins(c)
        pp = c.paragraphs[0]; no_space(pp); add_run(pp, cat, size=9, bold=True)
        cnt = {"R": 0, "Y": 0, "G": 0}
        for j, st in enumerate(stvals):
            cnt[st] += 1
            cc = tbl.cell(r, j + 1); cc.width = widths[j + 1]; shade(cc, fill[st]); set_cell_margins(cc)
            pp = cc.paragraphs[0]; pp.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER; no_space(pp)
            add_run(pp, word[st], size=8, bold=True, color=fcolor[st])
        cc = tbl.cell(r, 8); cc.width = widths[8]; set_cell_margins(cc)
        pp = cc.paragraphs[0]; pp.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER; no_space(pp)
        add_run(pp, f"{cnt['R']}/{cnt['Y']}/{cnt['G']}", size=9, bold=True)
    # total row
    r = len(cats) + 1
    c = tbl.cell(r, 0); c.width = widths[0]; shade(c, NAVY_HEX); set_cell_margins(c)
    pp = c.paragraphs[0]; no_space(pp); add_run(pp, "TOTAL (all dimensions)", size=9, bold=True, color=WHITE)
    tot = {"R": 0, "Y": 0, "G": 0}
    state_tot = {s: {"R": 0, "Y": 0, "G": 0} for s in states}
    for cat, stvals in cats:
        for j, st in enumerate(stvals):
            tot[st] += 1
            state_tot[states[j]][st] += 1
    for j, s in enumerate(states):
        cc = tbl.cell(r, j + 1); cc.width = widths[j + 1]; set_cell_margins(cc)
        st = state_tot[s]
        dom = "R" if st["R"] > 0 else ("Y" if st["Y"] > 0 else "G")
        shade(cc, fill[dom])
        pp = cc.paragraphs[0]; pp.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER; no_space(pp)
        add_run(pp, f"{st['R']}R {st['Y']}Y {st['G']}G", size=8, bold=True, color=fcolor[dom])
    cc = tbl.cell(r, 8); cc.width = widths[8]; shade(cc, NAVY_HEX); set_cell_margins(cc)
    pp = cc.paragraphs[0]; pp.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER; no_space(pp)
    add_run(pp, f"{tot['R']}/{tot['Y']}/{tot['G']}", size=9, bold=True, color=WHITE)

    cap = doc.add_paragraph()
    cap.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(3)
    cap.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(8)
    add_run(cap, "Figure 1. Compliance posture by category and jurisdiction (dimension-level counts). "
                 "See the Matrix for dimension-by-dimension detail.", size=8.5, italic=True, color=GREY)

    # ===================== IV. CRITICAL DEFICIENCIES =====================
    heading(doc, "IV.  Critical Deficiencies", 1)
    body(doc,
         "Critical deficiencies involve active legal exposure, an imminent regulatory deadline at risk, or a deficiency "
         "that has already resulted in, or is likely to imminently result in, regulatory action, penalties, or "
         "litigation. Four Critical items are identified.")

    def deficiency_block(doc, gid, title_text, status_color, status_word, narrative_paras, remediation_items):
        p = doc.add_paragraph()
        p.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(8)
        p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(3)
        add_run(p, f"{gid}  |  {title_text}", size=11, bold=True, color=NAVY)
        # status tag
        pt = doc.add_paragraph()
        pt.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(4)
        add_run(pt, "Severity: ", size=9.5, bold=True)
        add_run(pt, status_word, size=9.5, bold=True, color=status_color)
        for para in narrative_paras:
            body(doc, para, size=10.5, space_after=5)
        pr = doc.add_paragraph()
        pr.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(2)
        add_run(pr, "Recommended remediation:", size=10, bold=True, color=STEEL)
        for it in remediation_items:
            bullet(doc, it, size=10)

    deficiency_block(doc, "C-1", "Massachusetts PFML Registration — Imminent Deadline",
                     RED, "CRITICAL",
        ["The Company has not registered with the Massachusetts Paid Family and Medical Leave (MA PFML) program "
         "administered by the Massachusetts Department of Family and Medical Leave. MA PFML requires employer "
         "registration and contribution remittance from the first day any covered employee works in the Commonwealth. "
         "The Worcester facility is scheduled to open on August 1, 2025 with a projected 130 employees and $7.8 million "
         "in annual payroll.",
         "PinnacleHR has confirmed that it cannot configure payroll withholding until the Company provides its employer "
         "registration confirmation, and that its configuration lead time is approximately six to eight weeks from "
         "receipt of that confirmation. Working backward from August 1, 2025, registration must be completed no later "
         "than approximately June 1, 2025. As of the date of this memorandum, fewer than two weeks of slack remain "
         "against that date. Any slippage will cascade into a failure to have lawful withholding operational on the "
         "facility's opening day, exposing the Company to contribution delinquency, interest, and penalties under "
         "M.G.L. c. 175M. MA PFML also covers an employee's own serious health condition (up to 20 weeks of medical "
         "leave) and family leave (up to 12 weeks), at up to 80% of average weekly wage capped at approximately "
         "$1,170.64 per week (2025)—benefits the Company's self-funded plan does not approach."],
        ["Initiate MA PFML employer registration with the DFML immediately; treat as the single highest-priority "
         "registration item.",
         "Provide registration confirmation and employer account numbers to PinnacleHR no later than June 1, 2025 to "
         "preserve the configuration window.",
         "Confirm whether the Company will participate in the state program or pursue an approved private plan; given "
         "the equivalency analysis in Section VII, participation in the state program is the lower-risk path.",
         "Update the PTO payout policy (see H-7) and the sick leave waiting period (see H-3) before the first "
         "Massachusetts hire."])

    deficiency_block(doc, "C-2", "Colorado FAMLI Registration — Triggered by April 2025 Remote Hires",
                     RED, "CRITICAL",
        ["The Company has not registered with the Colorado Family and Medical Leave Insurance (FAMLI) program. Three "
         "salaried Colorado-based employees were hired to work remotely in April 2025—approximately seven months before "
         "the Aurora facility opens on November 1, 2025. Colorado FAMLI is mandatory for employers with ten or more "
         "employees and, critically, does not permit private-employer opt-out (the limited opt-out provision is "
         "available only to local government employers). The Company therefore cannot self-fund in lieu of FAMLI "
         "participation for any Colorado employee.",
         "If the FAMLI registration obligation is triggered by the first date a Colorado-based employee performs work "
         "for the Company—irrespective of whether a physical facility is operational—then the Company's registration "
         "may already be overdue as of the date of this memorandum. Even under the more favorable interpretation that "
         "the obligation attaches to the November 1, 2025 facility opening, the six-to-eight-week PinnacleHR lead time "
         "requires registration to be completed by approximately September 1, 2025. H&C recommends resolving the "
         "trigger-date question in favor of the earliest date (the April remote hires) and registering immediately. "
         "FAMLI provides up to 12 weeks of leave (16 for pregnancy/childbirth complications) at 90% of average weekly "
         "wage up to 50% of the state average weekly wage, then 50% above, capped at approximately $1,100 per week "
         "(2025)."],
        ["Register with the Colorado FAMLI Division immediately; do not await the November facility opening.",
         "Obtain a definitive legal determination on the registration trigger date for the April 2025 remote hires and "
         "remediate any contribution delinquency for the period since their start dates.",
         "Plan for mandatory state-program participation; abandon any self-funded alternative for Colorado employees.",
         "Coordinate PinnacleHR configuration to be operational no later than November 1, 2025."])

    deficiency_block(doc, "C-3", "New Jersey Earned Sick Leave Act Violations — Active NJ DOL Complaint",
                     RED, "CRITICAL",
        ["The Company is the respondent in an active complaint before the New Jersey Department of Labor and Workforce "
         "Development, Case No. NJ-ESL-2025-04117 (Carla Muñoz v. Vantage Logistics Group, Inc.), received February 3, "
         "2025. The complaint alleges that the Company denied Ms. Muñoz the use of accrued earned sick leave to care "
         "for her domestic partner's child, on the ground that the Company's policy limits covered family members to "
         "\"spouse, child, or parent.\" The New Jersey Earned Sick Leave Act, N.J.S.A. 34:11D-1 et seq., defines "
         "\"family member\" far more broadly to include a domestic partner, a child of a domestic partner, a civil "
         "union partner, grandparents, grandchildren, siblings, any other blood relative, and any individual whose "
         "close association with the employee is the equivalent of a family relationship.",
         "The Company's policy deficiencies extend well beyond the family-member definition. The policy enumerates "
         "covered uses limited to the employee's own illness, injury, or medical appointment, omitting the care of "
         "family members, absences related to domestic or sexual violence, public health emergency closures, and "
         "school-related functions—all of which are required uses under N.J.S.A. 34:11D-3. In addition, the policy's "
         "accrual rate (1 hour per 40 hours worked) is below the statutory rate (1 hour per 30 hours worked); the "
         "part-time accrual cap (24 hours) is below the 40-hour cap required for all employees; and the no-carryover "
         "rule violates the Act's carryover requirement (absent frontloading or year-end payout). The Department has "
         "reserved the right to expand its investigation beyond the Muñoz claim, which means these systemic defects "
         "are exposed to agency scrutiny in a live proceeding."],
        ["Amend the Paid Sick Leave policy to (i) adopt the full statutory definition of \"family member,\" (ii) "
         "enumerate all statutorily required covered uses, (iii) correct the accrual rate to 1 hour per 30 hours "
         "worked, (iv) raise the part-time cap to 40 hours, and (v) implement carryover (or frontloading) consistent "
         "with the Act.",
         "Make Ms. Muñoz whole for the unpaid time off or expended PTO resulting from the denial, as part of the "
         "resolution strategy in the NJ DOL proceeding (addressed in Section X).",
         "Conduct mandatory supervisor and HR training on the proper administration of earned sick leave, including "
         "identification of covered family members and permissible leave purposes, at the Edison and Newark facilities.",
         "Target policy amendment for the July 1, 2025 revised Handbook; implement training on a parallel track."])

    deficiency_block(doc, "C-4", "Self-Funded Paid Family Leave Plan — Below Statutory Minimums in All Seven States",
                     RED, "CRITICAL",
        ["The Company self-funds its paid family leave benefit at 50% of the employee's average weekly wage, capped at "
         "$850 per week, for up to eight weeks, covering bonding and family caregiving only. The Company has not "
         "obtained private-plan approval in any jurisdiction and does not participate as the contributing employer in "
         "any state family leave insurance program; PinnacleHR withholds only the employee-side contributions required "
         "by New Jersey, New York, and Connecticut.",
         "The self-funded benefit is below the statutory minimum in all seven jurisdictions on every material "
         "dimension—wage replacement rate, weekly benefit cap, and duration—and it omits qualifying military exigency, "
         "organ and bone-marrow donation, and (in several states) safe leave and the employee's own medical condition. "
         "Because no private-plan application has been filed, the plan does not qualify as a compliant substitute in "
         "any state that permits one (New Jersey, New York, Connecticut, Massachusetts, Oregon, and Washington), and "
         "it is categorically impermissible in Colorado, which does not permit private-employer opt-out. The practical "
         "result is that employees in the current three states are paying into state programs through payroll "
         "withholdings while the Company maintains a separate, inferior self-funded benefit—a structure that creates "
         "both under-delivery risk (employees receiving less than the state benefit) and dual-coverage confusion. "
         "Section VII analyzes the structural options in detail."],
        ["Make a structural decision by July 1, 2025 among: (a) participate in all state programs and discontinue or "
         "reposition the self-funded plan as a supplemental benefit; (b) file private-plan approvals in the six states "
         "that permit them, with benefits enhanced to meet or exceed each state's statutory minimum in all respects; "
         "or (c) a hybrid approach. H&C's preliminary recommendation is option (a) for operational simplicity and "
         "lowest residual risk, with the self-funded plan either discontinued or clearly framed as supplemental.",
         "For Colorado, plan for mandatory state-program participation (no opt-out available).",
         "Reconcile the current-state withholdings so that employees are unambiguously entitled to the full state "
         "benefit and are routed to the state program for claims (see H-8).",
         "Broaden the family-member definition and qualifying reasons in any retained self-funded or supplemental plan "
         "to match each state program."])

    # ===================== V. HIGH DEFICIENCIES =====================
    heading(doc, "V.  High-Severity Deficiencies", 1)
    body(doc,
         "High-severity deficiencies are likely to result in enforcement action, monetary penalties, or employee claims "
         "if not remediated before operations commence in the applicable jurisdiction or within the near term. Nine "
         "High items are identified.")

    high_items = [
        ("H-1", "New York Paid Sick Leave",
         "New York requires employers with 100 or more employees to provide up to 56 hours of paid sick leave per year "
         "(NY Labor Law § 196-b); the Company has 680 employees in New York. The policy provides 40 hours (full-time) "
         "and 24 hours (part-time), accrues at 1 hour per 40 hours worked (versus the statutory 1 per 30), imposes a "
         "120-day waiting period (New York has none), prohibits carryover, defines family members narrowly, and omits "
         "required covered uses. Each of these is independently non-compliant.",
         ["Increase the New York sick leave cap to 56 hours; correct the accrual rate to 1 hour per 30 hours worked; "
          "add carryover; broaden the family-member definition; enumerate all required covered uses; and remove the "
          "waiting period for New York employees. Target the July 1, 2025 revised Handbook."]),
        ("H-2", "Connecticut Paid Sick Leave",
         "Connecticut requires employers with 50 or more employees to provide up to 40 hours of paid sick leave per "
         "year (C.G.S. § 31-57s), with no distinction between full-time and part-time employees for the accrual cap. "
         "The Company's 24-hour part-time cap is non-compliant. The policy also prohibits carryover, defines family "
         "members narrowly, and omits required covered uses. (The accrual rate of 1 hour per 40 hours worked is "
         "consistent with Connecticut law.)",
         ["Raise the part-time cap to 40 hours; add carryover; broaden the family-member definition; and enumerate all "
          "required covered uses. Target the July 1, 2025 revised Handbook."]),
        ("H-3", "Massachusetts Earned Sick Time",
         "Massachusetts requires employers with 11 or more employees to provide up to 40 hours of paid sick time per "
         "year (M.G.L. c. 149 § 148C), accruing at 1 hour per 30 hours worked, available for use after 90 days, with "
         "carryover of up to 40 hours. The policy's accrual rate (1 per 40), 120-day waiting period, no-carryover rule, "
         "part-time cap, narrow family-member definition, and omitted covered uses are each non-compliant.",
         ["Correct the accrual rate to 1 per 30; reduce the waiting period to 90 days; add carryover; broaden the "
          "family-member definition; and enumerate required covered uses. Complete before the August 1, 2025 facility "
          "opening."]),
        ("H-4", "Colorado Healthy Families and Workplaces Act (Paid Sick Leave)",
         "Colorado requires all employers to provide paid sick leave at 1 hour per 30 hours worked, up to 48 hours per "
         "year, available for use immediately (no waiting period), with carryover, and including bereavement and "
         "supplemental public health emergency leave of up to 80 hours. The policy's 40/24-hour caps, 1-per-40 accrual, "
         "120-day waiting period, no-carryover rule, narrow family-member definition, and omitted covered uses are each "
         "non-compliant.",
         ["Set the cap at 48 hours; correct the accrual rate to 1 per 30; eliminate the waiting period; add carryover; "
          "broaden the family-member definition; and enumerate covered uses including bereavement and public health "
          "emergency supplemental leave. Complete before the November 1, 2025 facility opening."]),
        ("H-5", "Oregon Sick Time",
         "Oregon requires employers with 10 or more employees to provide up to 40 hours of paid sick time per year "
         "(ORS 653.601), accruing at 1 hour per 30 hours worked, available for use after 90 days, with carryover of up "
         "to 40 hours, and including safe leave (domestic violence, sexual assault, stalking, and harassment). The "
         "policy's accrual rate, 120-day waiting period, no-carryover rule, part-time cap, narrow family-member "
         "definition, and omitted covered uses (including safe leave) are non-compliant.",
         ["Correct the accrual rate to 1 per 30; reduce the waiting period to 90 days; add carryover; broaden the "
          "family-member definition; and enumerate covered uses including safe leave. Complete before the February 1, "
          "2026 facility opening."]),
        ("H-6", "Washington Paid Sick Leave",
         "Washington requires all employers to provide paid sick leave at 1 hour per 40 hours worked (RCW 49.46.210), "
         "available for use after 90 days, with no accrual cap and mandatory year-to-year carryover of all unused "
         "leave. The policy's accrual rate is consistent with Washington law, but its 120-day waiting period, accrual "
         "cap, no-carryover rule, narrow family-member definition, and omitted covered uses are non-compliant.",
         ["Reduce the waiting period to 90 days; remove the accrual cap and implement year-to-year carryover; broaden "
          "the family-member definition; and enumerate required covered uses. Complete before the May 1, 2026 facility "
          "opening."]),
        ("H-7", "PTO/Vacation Payout on Separation — MA, CO, OR, WA",
         "The Company's policy forfeits accrued, unused PTO on separation in all states except Connecticut. That "
         "forfeiture is unlawful in four jurisdictions: Massachusetts (M.G.L. c. 149 § 148, with treble damages under "
         "the Wage Act), Colorado (C.R.S. § 8-4-101, which prohibits use-it-or-lose-it for earned vacation), Oregon "
         "(ORS 652.140, treating accrued vacation as wages), and Washington (RCW 49.48.010, treating accrued vacation "
         "as a vested wage). The five-day carryover cap and the March 31 use-or-forfeit deadline may be scrutinized in "
         "these states as mechanisms to minimize the payout obligation. Waiting-time penalties for late final pay "
         "apply in each.",
         ["Amend the PTO policy to require payout of all accrued, unused vacation on separation in MA, CO, OR, and WA; "
          "review the carryover cap against each state's wage/vacation rules; and coordinate final-pay timing to avoid "
          "waiting-time penalties. The Massachusetts amendment must be in place before the first Massachusetts hire "
          "(by July 1, 2025); Colorado before the November 1, 2025 opening; Oregon and Washington before their "
          "respective openings."]),
        ("H-8", "Paid Medical Leave — Misdirection of Employees to Voluntary STD",
         "The Company provides no employer-funded paid medical leave for an employee's own serious health condition in "
         "any state, beyond accrued sick leave (capped at 40 hours for full-time employees) and PTO. Employees in New "
         "Jersey, New York, and Connecticut fund state disability and paid leave programs through payroll withholdings "
         "(NJ TDI, NY DBL, CT PL), each of which covers the employee's own condition. HR staff have nonetheless "
         "directed employees who inquired about paid medical leave to the voluntary, employee-paid short-term "
         "disability plan administered by Keystone Benefit Administrators, rather than to the state programs. At least "
         "seven such inquiries have been identified across Connecticut and New Jersey in the last twelve months, and "
         "fewer than 30% of Connecticut hourly employees are enrolled in the voluntary plan. This misdirection creates "
         "exposure to retroactive claims and mirrors the pattern that produced the Muñoz complaint—employees who "
         "believe they were denied a benefit they funded are willing to file with state agencies.",
         ["Issue immediate interim guidance directing HR staff at all locations to route own-condition claims to the "
          "applicable state program (NJ TDI, NY DBL, CT PL) and to escalate uncertainty to the Director of HR rather "
          "than defaulting to the voluntary plan.",
          "Compile and audit the full list of prior own-condition inquiries (CT, NJ, NY) to identify employees who may "
          "have been misdirected and assess retroactive claim exposure.",
          "Clarify, in the revised Handbook, the relationship between the state programs, the voluntary STD plan, and "
          "any retained self-funded benefit.",
          "Train HR staff on the distinction between mandatory state programs and the voluntary supplemental plan."]),
        ("H-9", "Oregon and Washington Program Registration",
         "The Company has not registered with Paid Leave Oregon (administered by the Oregon Employment Department) or "
         "the Washington Paid Family and Medical Leave program (administered by the Washington Employment Security "
         "Department). Accounting for the PinnacleHR configuration lead time, registration should be completed by "
         "approximately December 1, 2025 (Oregon) and March 1, 2026 (Washington) to ensure withholding is operational "
         "by the February 1, 2026 and May 1, 2026 facility openings, respectively. Both states permit private-plan "
         "alternatives, but any such plan must exceed the state benefit; the Company's current self-funded plan does "
         "not qualify.",
         ["Register with the Oregon Employment Department by December 1, 2025 and with the Washington Employment "
          "Security Department by March 1, 2026; provide confirmations to PinnacleHR; and determine participation "
          "versus an enhanced private plan consistent with the Section VII structural decision."]),
    ]
    for gid, title_text, narr, rem in high_items:
        p = doc.add_paragraph()
        p.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(8)
        p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(2)
        add_run(p, f"{gid}  |  {title_text}", size=10.5, bold=True, color=NAVY)
        pt = doc.add_paragraph(); pt.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(3)
        add_run(pt, "Severity: ", size=9.5, bold=True)
        add_run(pt, "HIGH", size=9.5, bold=True, color=DKYEL)
        body(doc, narr, size=10.5, space_after=4)
        pr = doc.add_paragraph(); pr.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(2)
        add_run(pr, "Recommended remediation:", size=10, bold=True, color=STEEL)
        for it in rem:
            bullet(doc, it, size=10)

    # ===================== VI. MODERATE DEFICIENCIES =====================
    heading(doc, "VI.  Moderate-Severity Deficiencies", 1)
    body(doc,
         "Moderate deficiencies are technical non-compliance, best-practice gaps, or policy ambiguities that may not "
         "give rise to immediate enforcement but expose the Company to risk over time or in the event of a complaint "
         "or audit. Seven Moderate items are identified.")

    mod_items = [
        ("M-1", "Paid Family Leave Notice — Foreseeable vs. Unforeseeable Leave",
         "The policy requires 30 calendar days' advance notice for all paid family leave and is silent on unforeseeable "
         "leave. Every state program (and the FMLA) requires only \"as soon as practicable\" notice when the need for "
         "leave is unforeseeable. The policy's silence has produced inconsistent application across facilities—some "
         "sites have enforced 30 days strictly while others have made case-by-case judgment calls—creating disparate-"
         "treatment and discrimination risk. A Connecticut employee who gave 10 days' notice for an unforeseeable "
         "parental hospitalization was initially challenged under the 30-day rule. Interim guidance has been issued; "
         "the policy must be rewritten to distinguish foreseeable from unforeseeable notice for each state."),
        ("M-2", "Paid Sick Leave Medical Documentation Threshold",
         "The policy requires a healthcare provider's note after two consecutive scheduled workdays of absence. Most "
         "states (New Jersey, New York, Massachusetts, Colorado, Oregon, Washington) permit an employer to require "
         "documentation only after three or more consecutive days; Connecticut permits documentation after two. The "
         "policy is therefore more stringent than permitted in six states, which could be challenged as an "
         "impermissible restriction on the use of earned leave. The threshold should be adjusted to three consecutive "
         "days (except Connecticut)."),
        ("M-3", "Workplace Posting and Individual Employee Notice Obligations",
         "The Handbook does not document compliance with the state-specific workplace posting and individual hire-notice "
         "obligations that apply in all seven jurisdictions for paid sick leave and the state paid leave insurance "
         "programs. Failure to post or to provide required individual notices is an independent violation in most "
         "states and is commonly cited in agency investigations. The Company should obtain and display all required "
         "postings, implement an individual notice process at hire for each state, and verify current-state postings "
         "immediately."),
        ("M-4", "Recordkeeping — Manual Spreadsheet Leave Tracking System",
         "The Company tracks sick leave accruals, usage, and balances through manual Excel workbooks maintained by two "
         "HR coordinators, one per state. The system lacks state-specific rule differentiation, automated alerts for "
         "accrual caps and carryover deadlines, an audit trail, and integration with the HRIS (WorkBridge Pro) and the "
         "time-and-attendance system (ClockSync Enterprise). Recent internal audits identified errors, including a "
         "part-time employee's balance incorrectly capped at 24 hours, negative balances from data-entry errors, and "
         "the 120-day waiting period applied uniformly without verification against state law. The system is not "
         "scalable to seven states and approximately 2,560 employees and creates recordkeeping risk in any audit or "
         "agency investigation. A dedicated leave-management solution should be selected and implemented (see Section "
         "VIII and the remediation plan)."),
        ("M-5", "Paid Family Leave Family-Member Definition and Qualifying Reasons",
         "Even setting aside the equivalency problem (C-4), the self-funded plan's family-member definition (spouse, "
         "child, parent, excluding parents-in-law) and qualifying reasons (bonding and caregiving only, omitting "
         "military exigency, organ and bone-marrow donation, and safe leave) are narrower than every state program. "
         "If the Company retains any self-funded or supplemental family leave benefit, these definitions must be "
         "broadened to match each state program; if the Company relies on the state programs, the policy must say so "
         "unambiguously."),
        ("M-6", "Connecticut Paid Sick Leave Use Threshold — Verification",
         "The Company applies a uniform 120-day waiting period in all states. The Connecticut paid sick leave use "
         "threshold under the 2025 statutory amendments should be verified against the policy; if Connecticut permits "
         "use earlier than 120 days, the policy should be adjusted for Connecticut employees. This item is classified "
         "Moderate pending confirmation of the current threshold."),
        ("M-7", "PTO Carryover Cap Scrutiny in Payout States",
         "The five-day carryover cap and the March 31 use-or-forfeit deadline may be scrutinized in Massachusetts, "
         "Colorado, Oregon, and Washington as mechanisms to minimize the vacation payout obligation at separation. "
         "While a carryover cap is not per se unlawful, its interaction with a no-payout policy in states that treat "
         "vacation as wages increases litigation risk. The cap should be reviewed against each state's wage and "
         "vacation rules and restructured where it could be characterized as a forfeiture device."),
    ]
    for gid, title_text, narr in mod_items:
        p = doc.add_paragraph()
        p.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(7)
        p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(2)
        add_run(p, f"{gid}  |  {title_text}", size=10.5, bold=True, color=NAVY)
        pt = doc.add_paragraph(); pt.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(3)
        add_run(pt, "Severity: ", size=9.5, bold=True)
        add_run(pt, "MODERATE", size=9.5, bold=True, color=STEEL)
        body(doc, narr, size=10.5, space_after=5)

    # ===================== VII. SELF-FUNDED PLAN ANALYSIS =====================
    heading(doc, "VII.  Self-Funded Paid Family Leave Plan — Structural Analysis", 1)
    body(doc,
         "The Company's self-funded paid family leave benefit (50% of average weekly wage, capped at $850 per week, for "
         "up to eight weeks, covering bonding and family caregiving) is the single most consequential structural issue "
         "in this review because it cuts across all seven jurisdictions and interacts with the contribution "
         "withholdings already in place. The table below compares the self-funded benefit against each state's "
         "statutory family leave benefit.")

    # Self-funded comparison table
    sf_rows = [
        ("New Jersey", "NJ FLI", "85% of AWW", "Statutory max", "12 wks", "Private plan permitted; none filed"),
        ("New York", "NY PFL", "67% of AWW", "$1,177.32 (2025)", "12 wks", "Private plan permitted; none filed"),
        ("Connecticut", "CT PL", "95%/60% tiered", "~$941 (2025)", "12 wks (+2)", "Private plan permitted; none filed"),
        ("Massachusetts", "MA PFML", "80%/50% tiered", "~$1,170.64 (2025)", "12 wks", "Private plan permitted; none filed"),
        ("Colorado", "CO FAMLI", "90%/50% tiered", "~$1,100 (2025)", "12 wks (+4)", "No private opt-out — must participate"),
        ("Oregon", "PLO", "100%/50% tiered", "~$1,523.63 (2025)", "12 wks (+2)", "Private plan permitted; none filed"),
        ("Washington", "WA PFML", "90%/50% tiered", "~$1,526 (2025)", "12 wks", "Voluntary plan permitted; none filed"),
    ]
    sf_tbl = doc.add_table(rows=1 + len(sf_rows) + 1, cols=6)
    sf_tbl.style = "Table Grid"
    sf_tbl.alignment = WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT.CENTER
    sf_w = [Inches(1.15), Inches(0.95), Inches(1.15), Inches(1.25), Inches(0.95), Inches(1.75)]
    sf_heads = ["State", "Program", "Wage replacement", "Weekly cap (2025)", "Duration", "Private-plan status"]
    for j, h in enumerate(sf_heads):
        c = sf_tbl.cell(0, j); c.width = sf_w[j]; shade(c, NAVY_HEX); set_cell_margins(c)
        pp = c.paragraphs[0]; pp.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER; no_space(pp)
        add_run(pp, h, size=8.5, bold=True, color=WHITE)
    set_repeat_header(sf_tbl.rows[0])
    for i, row in enumerate(sf_rows):
        r = i + 1
        for j, val in enumerate(row):
            c = sf_tbl.cell(r, j); c.width = sf_w[j]; set_cell_margins(c)
            if i % 2 == 1:
                shade(c, LGREY_HEX)
            pp = c.paragraphs[0]; no_space(pp)
            add_run(pp, val, size=8.5, bold=(j == 0))
    # Vantage row
    r = len(sf_rows) + 1
    vrow = ["Vantage (self-funded)", "—", "50% of AWW", "$850", "8 wks", "No approval filed; below all states"]
    for j, val in enumerate(vrow):
        c = sf_tbl.cell(r, j); c.width = sf_w[j]; shade(c, RED_HEX); set_cell_margins(c)
        pp = c.paragraphs[0]; no_space(pp)
        add_run(pp, val, size=8.5, bold=True, color=RED)
    cap = doc.add_paragraph(); cap.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(3); cap.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(8)
    add_run(cap, "Figure 2. Vantage self-funded PFL benefit versus state statutory family leave benefits. "
                 "The self-funded plan is below every state on rate, cap, and duration.", size=8.5, italic=True, color=GREY)

    body(doc,
         "Equivalency analysis. Six of the seven states (all except Colorado) permit an employer to substitute an "
         "approved private plan for state-program participation, but only if the private plan provides benefits equal "
         "to or greater than the state program in all respects—wage replacement rate, weekly benefit cap, duration, "
         "covered leave types, and family-member definitions. The Company's plan fails equivalency in every state on "
         "rate, cap, and duration alone, and no private-plan application has been filed in any jurisdiction. Colorado "
         "permits no private-employer opt-out at all. The self-funded plan therefore does not qualify as a compliant "
         "substitute in any state.")
    body(doc,
         "Structural options. H&C has identified three structural approaches, listed in order of H&C's preliminary "
         "preference for operational simplicity and lowest residual risk:")
    labeled_bullet(doc, "Option A — Participate in all state programs; discontinue or reposition the self-funded plan. ",
                   "The Company registers and participates in each state's program (mandatory in Colorado in any "
                   "event), withholds and remits both employee and employer contributions, and routes claims through "
                   "the state programs. The self-funded plan is either discontinued or clearly framed as a supplemental "
                   "benefit that tops up the state benefit. This eliminates equivalency risk and dual-coverage "
                   "confusion and is the most defensible posture.")
    labeled_bullet(doc, "Option B — File private-plan approvals in the six states that permit them, with enhanced benefits. ",
                   "The Company enhances the self-funded plan to meet or exceed each state's statutory minimum in all "
                   "respects and files for approval in New Jersey, New York, Connecticut, Massachusetts, Oregon, and "
                   "Washington. This preserves a uniform company-administered benefit but requires materially higher "
                   "benefit cost, state-by-state approval processes, and ongoing equivalency maintenance as state "
                   "benefits change annually. Colorado would still require state-program participation.")
    labeled_bullet(doc, "Option C — Hybrid. ",
                   "Participate in state programs in some states and maintain approved private plans in others, "
                   "calibrated to headcount, payroll, and administrative burden. This adds complexity and is "
                   "recommended only if a clear cost or operational advantage justifies it.")
    body(doc,
         "H&C's preliminary recommendation is Option A. It is the lowest-risk path, aligns with the contribution "
         "withholdings already in place in the current three states, and avoids the annual equivalency maintenance "
         "burden of Option B. The Company should make this decision by July 1, 2025 so that the revised Handbook and "
         "the PinnacleHR configurations for the expansion states can be built around a single, settled structure. "
         "Whichever option is selected, the family-member definitions and qualifying reasons in any retained "
         "self-funded or supplemental plan must be broadened to match each state program.")

    # ===================== VIII. CROSS-CUTTING OPERATIONAL RISKS =====================
    heading(doc, "VIII.  Cross-Cutting Operational Risks", 1)
    body(doc,
         "Three operational risks cut across all seven jurisdictions and amplify the legal deficiencies identified "
         "above. They are addressed together because no single policy amendment resolves them.")
    labeled_bullet(doc, "Manual spreadsheet leave tracking. ",
                   "The current one-workbook-per-state Excel system lacks state-specific rule differentiation, "
                   "automated alerts, an audit trail, and integration with the HRIS and time-and-attendance systems. "
                   "It has already produced documented errors (part-time cap misapplied, negative balances, uniform "
                   "application of the 120-day waiting period) and is not scalable to seven states and approximately "
                   "2,560 employees. H&C concurs with the Director of HR's recommendation to select and implement a "
                   "dedicated leave-management solution (estimated $40,000–$75,000, three-to-six-month implementation) "
                   "that integrates with WorkBridge Pro and ClockSync Enterprise and can be configured with state-"
                   "specific rules. This should be scoped against the $175,000 compliance remediation budget, with "
                   "consideration of a separate capital request if the budget is constrained.")
    labeled_bullet(doc, "HR claim-routing inconsistency. ",
                   "The misdirection of own-condition claims to the voluntary STD plan (H-8) reflects a broader "
                   "inconsistency in how HR staff across sites administer leave. The interim guidance issued by the "
                   "General Counsel is an appropriate stopgap, but a durable fix requires written procedures, training, "
                   "and a clear escalation path, all of which should be incorporated into the revised Handbook and HR "
                   "playbooks.")
    labeled_bullet(doc, "Notice and disparate-treatment risk. ",
                   "The 30-day notice rule's silence on unforeseeable leave (M-1) has produced inconsistent application "
                   "across facilities. Inconsistent administration of a facially uniform policy is a classic source of "
                   "disparate-treatment and discrimination claims and should be remediated through a rewritten, "
                   "state-specific notice provision and manager training.")

    # ===================== IX. PRIORITIZED REMEDIATION PLAN =====================
    heading(doc, "IX.  Prioritized Remediation Plan", 1)
    body(doc,
         "The following plan sequences remediation against the Company's facility opening schedule, the July 1, 2025 "
         "revised Handbook target, and the PinnacleHR configuration lead time. Owners are identified by function; "
         "specific personnel assignments are at the Company's discretion. Deadlines marked \"critical path\" will "
         "cascade into operational disruption if missed.")

    rem_rows = [
        ("Immediate\n(by May 2, 2025)", "C-1, C-2", "Initiate MA PFML and CO FAMLI registrations; obtain legal determination of CO registration trigger date for April remote hires.", "HR / Legal / PinnacleHR", "Critical path"),
        ("Immediate", "H-8", "Issue interim HR guidance: route own-condition claims to state programs (NJ TDI, NY DBL, CT PL); escalate uncertainty to Dir. of HR.", "HR (M. Delgado)", "High"),
        ("By June 1, 2025", "C-1", "Complete MA PFML registration; provide confirmation to PinnacleHR to preserve 6–8 wk configuration window before Aug 1 opening.", "HR / Legal / PinnacleHR", "Critical path"),
        ("By July 1, 2025", "C-3, C-4, H-1, H-2, M-1, M-2, M-5", "Publish revised Employee Handbook: amend PSL (family member, uses, accrual, caps, carryover, waiting periods, doc threshold by state); rewrite PFL notice (foreseeable/unforeseeable); make self-funded plan structural decision; broaden PFL family member & reasons.", "Legal (H&C) / HR", "High"),
        ("By July 1, 2025", "H-7, M-7", "Amend PTO payout policy for MA/CO/OR/WA; review carryover cap in payout states.", "Legal (H&C) / HR", "High"),
        ("By July 1, 2025", "M-4", "Issue RFP / begin vendor selection for dedicated leave-management software.", "HR / IT", "Moderate"),
        ("By July 1, 2025", "M-3", "Obtain and post all required state postings; implement individual hire-notice process for current states.", "HR", "Moderate"),
        ("Before Aug 1, 2025", "H-3, H-7", "MA sick leave (1/30 accrual, 90-day use, carryover) and MA PTO payout operational before Worcester opening.", "HR / Legal", "High"),
        ("Aug 1, 2025", "—", "Massachusetts facility opens — all registrations, withholding, policies, and postings operational.", "HR / PinnacleHR", "Critical path"),
        ("By Sept 1, 2025", "C-2", "Complete CO FAMLI registration (if not already done for remote hires); PinnacleHR configuration complete.", "HR / Legal / PinnacleHR", "Critical path"),
        ("Before Nov 1, 2025", "H-4, H-7", "CO sick leave (48 hrs, 1/30, no waiting period, carryover, bereavement/PHE) and CO PTO payout operational before Aurora opening.", "HR / Legal", "High"),
        ("Nov 1, 2025", "—", "Colorado facility opens — all systems operational.", "HR / PinnacleHR", "Critical path"),
        ("By Dec 1, 2025", "H-9", "Complete Paid Leave Oregon registration; provide confirmation to PinnacleHR.", "HR / Legal / PinnacleHR", "High"),
        ("Before Feb 1, 2026", "H-5, H-7", "OR sick time (1/30, 90-day, carryover, safe leave) and OR PTO payout operational before Tigard opening.", "HR / Legal", "High"),
        ("Feb 1, 2026", "—", "Oregon facility opens — all systems operational.", "HR / PinnacleHR", "Critical path"),
        ("By Mar 1, 2026", "H-9", "Complete WA PFML registration; provide confirmation to PinnacleHR.", "HR / Legal / PinnacleHR", "High"),
        ("By Q1 2026", "M-4", "Implement dedicated leave-management software, configured for all seven states, before OR/WA openings.", "HR / IT", "Moderate"),
        ("Before May 1, 2026", "H-6, H-7", "WA sick leave (90-day use, no accrual cap, carryover) and WA PTO payout operational before Kent opening.", "HR / Legal", "High"),
        ("May 1, 2026", "—", "Washington facility opens — all systems operational; conduct 30-day post-launch audit.", "HR / PinnacleHR", "Critical path"),
        ("Ongoing", "C-3, H-8", "Supervisor and HR training on revised policies; audit prior own-condition misdirection; support NJ DOL complaint resolution.", "HR / Legal", "High"),
    ]
    rt = doc.add_table(rows=1 + len(rem_rows), cols=5)
    rt.style = "Table Grid"
    rt.alignment = WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT.CENTER
    rw = [Inches(1.15), Inches(0.7), Inches(3.0), Inches(1.25), Inches(0.85)]
    rh = ["Deadline", "Ref.", "Action item", "Owner", "Priority"]
    for j, h in enumerate(rh):
        c = rt.cell(0, j); c.width = rw[j]; shade(c, NAVY_HEX); set_cell_margins(c)
        pp = c.paragraphs[0]; pp.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER; no_space(pp)
        add_run(pp, h, size=8.5, bold=True, color=WHITE)
    set_repeat_header(rt.rows[0])
    for i, row in enumerate(rem_rows):
        r = i + 1
        for j, val in enumerate(row):
            c = rt.cell(r, j); c.width = rw[j]; set_cell_margins(c)
            if i % 2 == 1:
                shade(c, LGREY_HEX)
            pp = c.paragraphs[0]; no_space(pp)
            if j == 4:
                pp.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
                col = RED if val == "Critical path" else (DKYEL if val == "High" else STEEL)
                add_run(pp, val, size=8, bold=True, color=col)
            else:
                add_run(pp, val, size=8.5, bold=(j == 0))
    cap = doc.add_paragraph(); cap.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(3); cap.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(8)
    add_run(cap, "Figure 3. Prioritized remediation plan sequenced to facility openings and the July 1, 2025 revised "
                 "Handbook target. \"Ref.\" cross-references the Gap Register and Sections IV–VI.", size=8.5, italic=True, color=GREY)

    # ===================== X. NJ DOL COMPLAINT =====================
    heading(doc, "X.  NJ DOL Complaint (Case No. NJ-ESL-2025-04117) — Resolution Posture", 1)
    body(doc,
         "The pending NJ DOL complaint is outside the scope of this engagement, which does not include representation "
         "of the Company in that proceeding (handled under the separate engagement dated February 15, 2025). However, "
         "the compliance review findings materially inform the Company's response posture, and H&C offers the following "
         "contextual observations for coordination with the team handling the complaint.")
    body(doc,
         "The complaint (C-3) targets the precise policy defects—family-member definition and covered-uses "
         "enumeration—that this review has confirmed are non-compliant under the New Jersey Earned Sick Leave Act. The "
         "Company's February 21, 2025 response acknowledged that the policy language \"may not fully reflect\" the Act "
         "and committed to a policy review by the July 1, 2025 Handbook revision. The remediation steps in C-3 "
         "(amending the policy, making Ms. Muñoz whole, and supervisor training) are consistent with that posture and "
         "with the relief sought in the complaint. The Department has reserved the right to expand its investigation, "
         "which means the systemic defects (accrual rate, part-time cap, no carryover) are exposed regardless of how "
         "the individual claim is resolved. Prompt, documented remediation therefore serves both the individual matter "
         "and the broader compliance posture, and the Company should ensure that the remediation record is preserved "
         "for use in the NJ DOL proceeding. H&C is available to coordinate with the team handling the complaint on the "
         "scope and timing of any remediation communications to the Department.")

    # ===================== XI. CONCLUSION =====================
    heading(doc, "XI.  Conclusion and Next Steps", 1)
    body(doc,
         "Vantage's paid leave policies and practices, as last revised in January 2022, do not comply with the paid "
         "leave requirements of any of the seven jurisdictions in which the Company operates or plans to operate. The "
         "deficiencies are systemic rather than incidental: the paid sick leave policy is non-compliant in all seven "
         "states; the self-funded paid family leave plan is below every state's statutory minimum and is not approved "
         "as a private-plan substitute anywhere; the Company provides no employer-funded paid medical leave for an "
         "employee's own condition in any state; the PTO payout policy is unlawful in four states; and the Company has "
         "not registered with the paid leave program in any of the four expansion states. The manual leave-tracking "
         "system and inconsistent HR administration amplify these legal risks.")
    body(doc,
         "The remediation path is achievable on the Company's timeline if action begins immediately. The two most "
         "time-sensitive items are the Massachusetts and Colorado program registrations, which are on or past the "
         "critical path. The July 1, 2025 revised Handbook is the central vehicle for curing the policy defects in the "
         "current states and for establishing compliant policies before the expansion facilities open. H&C recommends "
         "that the Company (1) initiate the Massachusetts and Colorado registrations this week; (2) make the self-"
         "funded plan structural decision (Section VII) by July 1, 2025; (3) commit to the revised Handbook timeline; "
         "and (4) authorize the leave-management software selection in parallel. H&C is available to present these "
         "findings to the legal and HR teams, to assist with the structural decision and registration filings, and to "
         "support the revised Handbook drafting under a separate engagement if the Company elects.")
    body(doc,
         "H&C will provide an interim status update on or about May 2, 2025. Given the registration urgency, the "
         "Company should anticipate that H&C may provide an early-action notification regarding Massachusetts and "
         "Colorado registration in advance of that update. This memorandum and the accompanying Matrix should be "
         "treated as privileged and confidential, and sharing with third-party vendors should be limited to non-"
         "privileged summaries or specific operational directives, as discussed in Section 7 of the engagement letter.",
         space_after=10)

    # Closing
    p = doc.add_paragraph(); p.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(10); p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(2)
    add_run(p, "Respectfully submitted,", size=10.5)
    p = doc.add_paragraph(); p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(0); no_space(p)
    add_run(p, "HARTWELL & CABOT LLP", size=10.5, bold=True, color=NAVY)
    p = doc.add_paragraph(); p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(0); no_space(p)
    add_run(p, "By:  Daniel Ikeda, Senior Associate", size=10.5)
    p = doc.add_paragraph(); p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(8); no_space(p)
    add_run(p, "Catherine Ng, Partner (supervising)", size=10.5)

    p = doc.add_paragraph(); p.paragraph_format.space_before = Pt(6)
    add_run(p, "cc:  Marcus Delgado, Director of Human Resources, Vantage Logistics Group, Inc.", size=9, color=GREY)
    p = doc.add_paragraph()
    add_run(p, "Enclosure:  Multi-State Compliance Matrix (multi-state-compliance-matrix.xlsx)", size=9, color=GREY)

    out = "output/gap-analysis-memo.docx"
    doc.save(out)
    print(f"Wrote {out}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    build()", "file_path": "build_memo.py"}
